Clinical: Angina pectoris; Catalepsy; Croup; Diabetes; Dyspnœa; Epilepsy; Fainting; Heart, failure of; Herpes, mercurial and venereal; Hiccough; Hypochondriasis; Hysteria; Hystero-epilepsy; Impotence; Laryngismus; Lungs, paralysis of; Pregnancy, complaints of; Rage, fits of; Stings; Typhoid; Vertigo; Whooping-cough

A sudden sensation in upper part of larynx as if it closed upon the breath; as if caused by the vapour of sulphur. Severe dry cough morning, pain under l. breast on coughing. Whooping-cough, last stage, with vertigo and constriction of chest and trachea. Difficult respiration, and shortness of breath, with shootings in chest. Cramp-like and suffocating constriction in chest, esp. after taking cold (or on going out into cold open air; or becoming cold). Hysterical spasms of chest. Cramps in chest, commencing sometimes with an inclination to cough, and afterwards aggravated so as to create despair. Pressive pain in chest, to extent of obstructing respiration. Gnawing in chest with sense of suffocation. Paralysis of lungs, loud rattling of mucus; restlessness; syncope. Shootings in chest and sides, sometimes with redness and bloatedness of face, dilated pupils, rough and dry tongue, and burning thirst. The chest is acutely affected, and painful on all sides, with violent and dry cough. Painful sensitiveness of chest under arms, esp. when pressed. A pressive pain in l. breast as though the pain would force itself through the nipple. Angina, tightness of chest, obliged to breathe forcibly. Drawing pains along the entire extent of the arm, and esp. in wrists, as from cramp. Burning, with sensation of coldness, in last joints of fingers. Swelling of hands, with shooting pains. Convulsive movements of hands and fingers. Restlessness in legs, with paralytic weakness, which compels constant movement. Trembling in legs, as after great fatigue, when seated. Compressive pain in hollows of knees as though tendons too short. Paralytic weakness in legs, on sitting down, after walking. Sensation of coldness on tibia. Burning pressure on tips of r. toes. Jerking pains in nails of two first toes as if they would suppurate, could not bear anything to touch them, could only walk barefooted upon the heel. Pricking in limbs, sometimes with pain in the part, affected. Cramp-like pains in limbs. Trembling and jerking, over whole body. Weakness, which is felt more during repose than in movement. Weakness to the extent of fainting, with nocturnal coldness of skin generally. Fainting fits: esp. at night (in bed), in evening, or in open air, followed by headache; of hysterical persons. Hypochondriacal complaints, originating in sexual system. Wrenching and bruising pain, in side on which patient has been lying. The sufferings are when the body is chilled. Great susceptibility to open air. Pinching, shootings, and itching, which compels scratching, in different parts of body. Tetanus. Convulsions with cramps in chest. Hysterical symptoms, even in men. [We may think strongly of this remedy in nervous or spasmodic complaints where the patient feels very cold; in epileptic fits, for instance, where there is rigor or chilliness, or shuddering, as though the patient were very cold. In complaints where the smell of musk affords great relief. Where there is a sense of fulness in the inner parts. Sensations: of coldness of the skin; great sensation of coldness in outer single parts; external chilliness with internal heat. Pain where there is a sensation of oppression, or a very severe oppressive pain. H. N. G.].

Pain in the umbilical region. [Morgenstern, |.c.]. In the right side of the abdomen, below the navel, simple pain. She feels too tight in the abdomen, without pain, with anxiety, so that she cannot do any work nor remain in one place, but must run about; she ran to several of her acquaintances, but remained with none above a few minutes (immediately). Loud rumbling without cessation in the abdomen, without flatulent sufferings; it ceases after a meal, and even while eating. (f-h1]. Diarrhea. [Morgenstern, l.c.]. He has urging to evacuation of flatus and stool; the stool is natural; before but not with the latter, a little flatus is expelled. Constipation for several days. Formication at the orifice of the rectum, which goes off on rubbing. It seems to excite the sexual desire. Excitation of the sexual desire. [Vogel, Hist. Mat. Med., p.356.-Piderit, Pharm. Rat., p.268.]. Exalted sexual power in a weak old man. [Weickhard, Med. Pract. Handbuch, Heilbronn und Rothenb., 1798, 1799.]. Promotes the menses. [Schroeck, l.c.]. Occurrence of the menses from the mere smell. [Vogel, |.c.-Th. Bartholin, Epist. Med., Cent. 11, p.87.]. Muriaticum acidum (Muriatic acid.) {mur.ac} (It should be carefully freed from the sulphuric acid often mixed with it, by means of redistillation over chloride of sodium, or (better) it may be precipitated by muriate of baryta, and, after being thus freed from the sulphuric acid, re-distilled.) {mur.ac} From vol. v, 2nd edit. {mur.ac} For medicinal use one drop of it is first diluted by means of two successions with 100 drops of diluted alcohol (made of equal parts of distilled water and strong alcohol shaken together ten times), and of this one drop is to be twice succussed (with two strokes of the arm) with 100 drops of undiluted alcohol (1/10000th), and then of this one drop is to be again twice shaken with 100 drops of alcohol (+). One globule the size of a poppy seed, moistened with this million-fold dilution, is given for a homeopathic dose. This represents the smallest portion of a drop, for with one drop 200 such globules are sufficiently moistened. Yet this million-fold dilution, although administered in such a small volume, will be found in many cases to be still too powerful when muriatic acid is homeopathically indicated, because this medicine possesses a high degree of efficacy. {mur.ac} Although a tolerably homeopathic employment in suitable morbid states can be made from the following observed alterations in the healthy, yet it would be desirable to possess a more complete proving of it as to its pure effects. {mur.ac} HAHNEMANN'S fellow-provers were GUTMANN, HARTMANN, HAYNEL, LANGHAMMER, STAPF, WISLICENUS. {mur.ac} Symptoms were obtained from the following old-school authorities: {mur.ac} CRAWFORD, in Sammnil. f. prakt. Pain in the pelvic region as if dislocated, on the slightest movement. {nux.v}. Tearing in the loins. {nux.v}. Drawing pain going up from the loins into the back, combined with a paralytic stiffness. {nux.v}. Immediately after the (evening) meal, aching pain in the loins up the spine, which causes anxiety (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. Dragging and tearing in the bottom of the back when walking and sitting, not when lying. {nux.v}. Dragging tearing backache. {nux.v}. Drawing pain in the back. {nux.v}. In the afternoon, a drawing in the back from the nape downwards (when sitting) and at the same time a violent pain like clawing in the scrobiculus cordis, so that she must sit in a bent position. {nux.v}. Drawing tearing pain in the back (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. Burning tearing backache. {nux.v}. Contractive, as it were constrictive backache. {nux.v}. Stiffness of the back (aft. some h.). {nux.v}. Aching pain in the spinal vertebrae (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. Bruised pain in the back; on touching and pressing on it more painful, as if congested with blood. {nux.v}. Pain as if bruised in the dorsal and abdominal muscles, even when touched (aft. 30 h.). {nux.v}. Pain in one scapula, as if sprained. {nux.v}. Drawing pain as if bruised betwixt the scapulae, especially when stooping forwards. {nux.v}. Constrictive pain betwixt the scapulae. {nux.v}. Pain on moving the head betwixt the scapulae and in the nape (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. A pain betwixt the scapulae as if bruised and drawing, especially when stooping forwards. {nux.v}. On the last cervical vertebra a pain, as if the flesh were torn away, he cannot even bear his shirt to touch it. {nux.v}. Cracking of the cervical vertebrae on moving the head (aft. 3 h.). {nux.v}. Pain as if bruised in the nape on moving (stooping) and on touching (aft. 6 h.). {nux.v}. The left side of the cervical muscles is swollen and painful when the head is moved, as if the tendons were too short and would not yield. {nux.v}. In the shoulder-joint and scapula, pain like a bruise on moving the head sideways towards the opposite side. {nux.v}. In the evening, in bed, pain in the left shoulder-joint, when he lies on the opposite side, as if the ligaments were torn, which goes off when he lies on the painful side (aft. 48 h.). {nux.v}. Pain in the shoulder-joint, as if paralysed and the whole arm heavy and tired, when sitting as well as when walking; after moving a little he cannot keep his arm up. {nux.v}. Rheumatic pain in the right shoulder and deltoid muscle. {nux.v}. In the head of the shoulder and arm sensation of warmth here and there. {nux.v}. After good sleep, she is very tired on rising in the morning; arms (and legs) are painful as if she had slept on a hard bed (after sitting quietly for half an hour she feels quite strong again). {nux.v}. On extending the arms a darting into the fingers like a spasm and pricks like needles. {nux.v}. Weight and weariness of arms (and legs) in the afternoon. {nux.v}. Sensation of a sudden powerlessness of the arms (and legs) in the morning (aft. 12h.). {nux.v}. Drawing pain in the arm. {nux.v}. Upward drawing pain in the arm with paralytic stiffness. {nux.v}. On walking in the open air, first a pain in the nape, which then extended into the wrist-joint, a paralytic pain as from weakness; he had no power of grasping firmly; going off in the evening when lying in bed. {nux.v}. Pale swelling of the hands and fingers (aft. 20 h.). {nux.v}. A burning on the back of the hands. {nux.v}. Twitching shooting pain backwards in the direction of the thumb bones. {nux.v}. On treading and walking a burning shooting from the sacrum through the thighs. {nux.v}. While walking an itching on the thighs. {nux.v}. Itching on the left thigh and foot, especially in the evening when he gets into bed. {nux.v}. A burning itching miliary rash on both thighs during the menses. {nux.v}. Tearing and shooting pain a little above and below the knee, in the evening (aft. 36 h.). {nux.v}. Weakness in the right lower extremity, when walking in the open air. {nux.v}. Heaviness and fatigue of legs (and arms), in the afternoon, especially when going up hill. {nux.v}. The legs are not able to support the body; he must lie down. {nux.v} (f-h1]. Sensation of sudden powerlessness of (arms and) legs, in the morning (aft. 12 h.). {nux.v}. Trembling of one knee and foot, along with an eager and even agreeable tension of the mind, for several evenings, when standing. {nux.v}. After walking in the open air a twitching in the houghs, when standing. {nux.v}. When rising up from a seat, sensation in the houghs as if they were too short. {nux.v}. Stiffness and tension in the hough, especially after standing (aft. 2 h.). {nux.v}. In both patellae a tensive pain as from a fatiguing journey, on going up stairs, worse in the morning. {nux.v}. Disagreeable sensation in the knee-joint on walking, as if the synovial fluid were deficient and it would crack. {nux.v}. Only in the day time pain in the knees, as if they were bruised, when moving and when at rest. {nux.v}. Painful swelling on the knee. {nux.v}. On the knee a miliary burning itching eruption. {nux.v}. Itching in the houghs in the morning; he must scratch. {nux.v}. A kind of small boil on the knee that makes the whole leg stiff. {nux.v}. Spasmodic drawing in the legs. {nux.v}. Going to sleep of the leg when sitting and standing, and when she touches it with the other leg, shooting in it. {nux.v}. Sensation in the leg as if asleep, but without prickling, followed by a sensation of contraction. {nux.v}. The leg goes to sleep after sitting, when walking and standing (aft. 18 h.). {nux.v}. Tearing pain in the left leg as far as the toes, in the afternoon (aft. 7 h.). {nux.v}. Tearing pain in the ulcer on the leg, when the open air comes in contact with it; which goes off when it is protected from the air by being covered up (aft. 4, 20 h.). {nux.v}. Inflammatory redness round the ulcer on the leg when walking and during other movement. {nux.v}. Itching of the leg at some distance from the ulcer. {nux.v}. Tensive pain in the calves. {nux.v}. A formication in the calves after walking in the open air. {nux.v}. A fixed, pricking burning pain on a small spot on the tibia (aft. 1/4 h.). {nux.v}. A formication from the feet upwards. {nux.v}. Tendency to dislocation of the ankle-joint and bending under him when walking (aft. 4 h.). {nux.v}. Painful, cramplike contraction of the soles when the legs are bent, which goes off on stretching out the legs. {nux.v}. In the soles, burning pain. {nux.v}. Pain as if the shoe were too tight and pinched, and as if the soles were tired and sore from walking. {nux.v}. An itching burning on the toes, as if they had been frost-bitten, in warm weather, especially when he comes into a warm room, or into bed. {nux.v}. After midnight, in bed, cramp of the toes. {nux.v}. (The pains increase in the evening from 8 to 9 o'clock, until they become intolerable.) {nux.v}. (Sensitiveness of the skin of the whole body, as if it were sore; on touching it, it feels as if that part of the skin had gone to sleep.) {nux.v}. Old wounds that were healed became again painful, like soreness (immediately). {nux.v}. Eruptions cause itching burning. {nux.v}. Itching eruptions. [Wiel, l.c.] {nux.v}. (Smarting) itching here and there, especially in the most external parts of the body limbs and joints, in the evening after lying down (aft. 4 h.). {nux.v}. Burning itching all over the body. {nux.v}. In the evening in bed, a burning itching all over the body. {nux.v}. Burning itching on the upper arms, thighs, abdomen and back, in the morning while dressing, in the evening while undressing, and even at night. {nux.v}. A burning pricking here and there on the body. {nux.v}. Here and there burning shooting or stitches which end in a burning. {nux.v}. A burning itching pricking (like needle-pricks) here and there in the skin, as from fleas, in the evening after lying down (aft. 5 1/2 d.). {nux.v} (f-h1]. Burning itching pricks on different parts of the body. {nux.v}. Single stitches on the affected parts from time to time. {nux.v}. Here and there on the body, single coarse stitches combined with a sore pain. {nux.v}. Stitches, like twitching, in various parts, so that the whole body is shaken by them; they dart as it were through the whole body (aft. 4 h.). {nux.v}. In the evening in bed, twitching in the limbs. {nux.v}. Trembling (aft. 2 h.). {nux.v}. Trembling of the limbs and palpitation of the heart (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. Tensive pain in the limbs, early in the morning, with stuffed nose (aft. 10 h.). {nux.v}. Diminished mobility of all the joints. {nux.v}. Violent contractive, painful sensation through the whole body. {nux.v}. Along with a painful contractive sensation through the whole body, a weariness in the lower extremities, so that he can scarcely drag them along. {nux.v}. A sensation in the muscles of the limbs, back, scapulae, c, as if something within drew to and fro, more spasmodic than painful. {nux.v} (f-h1]. Twitching and quivering in the limbs under the skin. {nux.v}. All the joints are more painful during movement than when lying still, after midnight (aft. 6 h.). {nux.v}. Pain in all the joints as if bruised, when moving (aft. 4 h.). {nux.v}. All her limbs are weary. {nux.v}. Gone-to-sleep feeling and insensibility (numbness) of almost all parts of the body. Attack, after midnight; she has formication on the hands and feet, this rises, with heat in the face, up to the heart (in the scrobiculus cordis), as if it burnt and ached there; it then mounts to the throat; she feels ill and anxious; thence it comes into the head; she feels stupid in the head and has ringing in the ears. {nux.v}. Attack, in the evening; it rises to the heart; he feels ill and anxious; he trembles and he must rest his head bent forwards on the table (aft. 4 d.). {nux.v}. Sudden attack soon after dinner; paleness of face; a nausea rises up from the scrobiculus cordis; he becomes anxious all over, with trembling and slight tremor through the whole body, with increasing exhaustion, so that he must lie down (aft. 8 d.). {nux.v}. When walking quickly in the open air congestion towards her head; she feels destitute of thinking power, must remain standing, the blood rushed to the heart, the upper part of the windpipe contracted, she had sparks of fire before the eyes; she did not see where she was. {nux.v}. In the morning, in the open air, her eyes because all at once staring; she lost consciousness and feeling, as in an attack of syncope, but only for a moment. {nux.v}. Great exhaustion after enjoying the open air, and sensation in the left foot as if it were stiff (aft. 6 h.). {nux.v}. A morning walk in the open air causes extraordinary fatigue. {nux.v}. Great fatigue of the whole body during a walk in the open air (aft. 28 h.). {nux.v}. After a walk in the open air very sad and uncommonly tired. {nux.v} (f-h1]. Exhaustion after a walk in the open air, in the evening. {nux.v} [f-h1l]. Great exhaustion and relaxation of all the limbs after enjoying the open air (aft. 8 h.). {nux.v}. Great weariness. {nux.v}. During the slightest exercise, immediately weariness. {nux.v}. Staggering gait, with fear of falling. [Veckoskrift, l.c.] {nux.v}. Weakness and staggering of the legs, he must sit down. Sudden sinking of the strength. [Matthiolus, l.c.] {nux.v}. She becomes thinner. {nux.v}. Heaviness in the arms and legs, so that she cannot lift either. {nux.v}. Sensation of sudden, as it were paralytic powerlessness in all the limbs, even when sitting, but mostly when moving (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. Qualmishness about the heart. {nux.v}. Syncope. {nux.v}. Attack of syncope in the evening (about 8 or 9 p.m.) while sitting. {nux.v}. In the afternoon great weakness with loss of appetite. {nux.v}. Great desire to sit down (aft. 6 h.). {nux.v}. The pains are relieved by lying down. {nux.v}. Desire to lie down; he cannot remain up. {nux.v}. In the forenoon desire to lie down. {nux.v}. In the morning desire to lie down again. {nux.v}. Great dislike to get up in the morning, without knowing why (aft. 12 h.). {nux.v}. Greater weariness in the morning after rising than in the evening when he went to bed. {nux.v}. Sleepiness not until the morning, after day dawn. {nux.v}. Sleepiness (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. She is always inclined to yawn and go to sleep in the daytime, so that she could not keep awake. {nux.v}. Uncommon drowsiness by day, as from stupefaction of the head. {nux.v}. When walking in the open air, first drowsiness, then palpitation of the heart and great anxiety, with swelling of the blood-vessels of the hands, without heat (aft. 36 h.). {nux.v}. Before dinner (about 11 a.m.) inclination to sleep. {nux.v}. After eating, almost irresistible sleepiness, for several hours (aft. 5 h.). {nux.v}. He dreams and speaks aloud in the noonday sleep. {nux.v}. Late of going to sleep in the evening (aft. 2 h.). {nux.v}. He falls asleep late in the evening, kept awake by great afflux of ideas. {nux.v}. Sleeplessness until midnight, with feeling of heat without thirst (aft. 12h.). {nux.v}. At night great restlessness without pain. {nux.v}. At night restlessness in the arms, which he must at one time cover up at another uncover. {nux.v}. In the evening, after lying down in bed, a restlessness and anxiety, so that he must always draw up and then stretch out the limbs (aft. 8 h.). {nux.v}. Before midnight restlessness in the lower extremities, an almost voluptuous, agreeable, but intolerable sensation in them, which prevents him going to sleep, constantly wakes him up when he wishes to go to sleep, and compels him to draw up and stretch out his legs alternately. {nux.v}. Very sweet, almost unconquerable slumber till late in the morning (aft. 20 h.). {nux.v}. In the morning hard to waken. {nux.v}. He can only sleep before midnight, from 11 to 1 o'clock, he then wakes up and must get up at 3 o'clock. {nux.v}. Great sleepiness with yawning, in the evening, two hours before the usual time for sleep; in bed he goes to sleep immediately, lies awake for a long time after midnight, then sleeps until late in the morning, with vivid dreams full of events of the previous day, and in the morning he will not get up from bed. {nux.v}. When going to sleep he starts up in affright. {nux.v}. Starting at night in sleep and by day when awake. {nux.v}. Frightened starting in sleep so that he does not awake to full consciousness. {nux.v}. At the slightest noise he wakes with a start. {nux.v}. In the afternoon siesta a start and jerk through the whole body, like an electric shock; as though he would fall to the ground. {nux.v}. (In the evening slumber he leaps out of bed in a delirious state.) {nux.v}. (Anxious delirious phantasies, in the evening in bed (about 9 p.m.), as if some one were coming into bed beside him, and there is no room, his bed has been sold, c.) {nux.v}. He often wakes up at night, and cannot readily go again to sleep, if he does sleep, he dreams very vivid dreams. {nux.v}. Frightful visions in his dreams, causing fear. {nux.v}. At night, when half-awake, sad phantasies; e.g. of trunkless heads of deceased acquaintances. {nux.v}. She cannot sleep at night, and when she slumbers a bit she has frightful dreams, which wake her up; she remains awake for hours, and when she again falls asleep she has other frightful dreams and on waking knows what she has dreamt. {nux.v}. Frightful delirious ravings at night. {nux.v}. Dreams that excite horror (e.g. of wild beasts). {nux.v}. Dreams of diseased or maimed human beings. {nux.v}. Wakes up at night from horrible dreams (aft. 10 h.). {nux.v}. Dreams of lice and vermin. {nux.v}. Dreams that all his teeth fell out of his mouth. {nux.v}. Dreams of business matters that require the greatest attention. {nux.v}. Disagreeable dreams about things that had happened or been talked of the previous day. {nux.v}. Quite early in the morning (about 4 a.m.) an anxious whining talking in sleep, followed by discharge of flatus (aft. 10 h.). {nux.v}. Very anxious dreaming and weeping in sleep. {nux.v}. Early waking with apprehensiveness. {nux.v}. On waking in the morning anxiety with ebullition of the blood and depression of spirits, which both go off when he gets up. {nux.v}. Groaning whining in sleep. {nux.v}. In his sleep before midnight, muttering of incomprehensible words, sometimes in peevish or complaining tone. {nux.v}. At night half-waking dreams, accompanied with fatiguing thoughts (aft. a few h.). {nux.v}. His sleep is unquiet and full of cares. {nux.v}. Indifference to cruel lacerations and mutilations witnessed in a dream (aft. 6h.). {nux.v}. The night seems to him to be very long and tedious, with a kind of soporous stupefaction (coma), with dreams full of urgent business. {nux.v}. When asleep he generally lies on his back, with one or other arm elevated and laid under the head. {nux.v}. Lying at night on the back with one or both arms stretched above the head; he talks in his sleep and wakes up between 2 and 3 a.m. {nux.v}. In his sleep he lies on his back with head thrown back, the arms above the head, so that his hands lie under his nape. {nux.v}. In his sleep he always tries to lie on his back and with his head as low as possible (aft. 36 h.). {nux.v}. Before midnight in sleep snoring inspiration, as if the posterior orifices of his nose or the soft palate were contracted and narrowed. {nux.v} (f-h1]. Loud snorting breathing in sleep before midnight. {nux.v}. Loud blowing and whistling expiration through the nose in sleep (aft. 4h.). {nux.v}. In the morning in bed he does not feel well; he fears to get up, as if over-tired by a long walk, which went off on rising. {nux.v}. Very convulsive stretching and straining. [Bergius, l.c.] {nux.v}. Much yawning and stretching, in the afternoon. {nux.v}. Very frequent stretching and straining which seems to do her good. {nux.v}. In the morning uncommon stretching of the limbs and yawning, and after the stretching a spasmodic pain in the limbs, especially the knee. {nux.v}. In the morning in bed a stretching with arms extended outwards, which seems to have its origin in the abdomen. {nux.v}. Long attack of continued yawning, which leaves great exhaustion (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. Whilst yawning, in the morning, the eyes fill with water and weep. {nux.v}. In the morning, immediately after rising from bed, yawning (aft. 16 h.). {nux.v}. In the morning, immediately after yawning, headache. {nux.v}. Yawning which excites coughing. {nux.v}. In the morning after rising (and drinking) diarrheic stool, then exhaustion, yawning, drowsiness, chilliness, confusion of the head then refreshing sleep (aft. 18 h.). {nux.v}. After the stretching and yawning spasmodic pains in the limbs, with chilliness and inward tremor. {nux.v}. During the yawning, shivering. {nux.v}. After the shivering, sleep, followed by shivering and coldness of the toes (aft. 16 h.). {nux.v}. After lying down, in the evening, chill over the back and arms (but not the hands) (aft. 3h.). {nux.v}. In the evening in bed she is chilly before going to sleep and when she wakes, it is as though she could not get warm in bed; not during the day. {nux.v}. At night tossing about and coldness, that is not dispelled by the heat of the bed. {nux.v}. He cannot get warm in bed at night. {nux.v}. Violent chilliness in bed at night, but towards morning sweat preceded by formication in the skin. {nux.v}. In the morning in bed excessive rigor, without externally perceptible coldness, for half an hour; followed by cramp like contraction of the toes and soles. {nux.v}. In the morning chilly feeling in the back and limbs, with painfulness of the skin as from exposure to cold and a sensation of going to sleep in the limbs, such as is caused by cold weather. {nux.v}. In the morning cold feet. {nux.v}. In the morning shivering and shuddering. {nux.v}. In the morning after rising chilliness, for several successive days. {nux.v}. In the afternoon sudden coldness either of the arms and hands or of the legs and feet, which is not dispelled by any movement. {nux.v} [f.-h1]. After drinking, immediately shivering and chilliness. {nux.v}. After vexation, chilliness in the back and heaviness of the lower extremities. {nux.v}. Chilliness on the slightest movement (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. From the slightest movement shivering all over the body, but not when lying still. {nux.v}. On the slightest exposure to the open air shivering and chilliness for an hour (with pain in the back) (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. On the least exposure to the open air chill and toothache, like fine or fine burning stitches. {nux.v}. He dreads to go into the open air (aft. 1/2 h.). {nux.v}. By the slightest draught of air he gets chilled (disagreeable feeling in the skin, bellyache, c.) (aft. a few h.). {nux.v}. Chilliness. {nux.v}. He cannot get warm. {nux.v}. Great coldness not removed by the heat of the stove nor by bed coverings. {nux.v} [fhl1]. Coldness of all the body, with blueness of the skin (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. Coldness of all the body, with blue hands, without goose-skin. {nux.v}. The temperature of the body is diminished all over the body (he loses heat). {nux.v}. Severe chilliness with chattering of the teeth. {nux.v}. Sensation of cold running over the face. {nux.v}. Sensation of cold about the head from time to time. {nux.v}. (Coldness) chilly sensation on the face and head. {nux.v}. Chilliness on the feet as if sprinkled with cold water, with trembling {nux.v}. Great coldness, at least of the limbs, without thirst. {nux.v}. Chill without thirst. {nux.v}. Thirst for small beer during the shivering (aft. 2 h.). {nux.v}. During the chill thirst for beer (aft. 24 h.). {nux.v}. An attack as of fever : shivering and drawing in the limbs, as if proceeding from pain in the sacrum, while lying in slumber, during the noonday siesta-not followed by heat, and without thirst. {nux.v}. An attack as of fever : at night (at 2 a.m.) intolerable drawing pain through thighs and legs, so that he could not compose himself, with thirst. {nux.v}. Nocturnal febrile attack (at 3 a.m.); before the chill intolerable drawing pain through thighs and legs, compelling him to draw them up and stretch them out alternately. {nux.v}. Without thirst and without sensation of heat, indeed, even during recurrence of the chilly feeling, violent heat of the body and redness of cheeks, except the hands, feet, and hairy scalp, which are cold. {nux.v}. Fever in the afternoon or evening; after the heat chill and coldness. {nux.v}. During the external or internal heat, at the same time chilliness and great exhaustion, which, especially in the afternoon, compel him to lie down and cover himself with the bedclothes, or at least put on warm clothes. {nux.v}. In the morning (about 6 a.m.) chill, occasionally combined with general heat and beads of perspiration on the forehead; then towards evening (6 p.m.) again chill. {nux.v}. In the evening redness of cheeks and heat of hands with cold feet and recurrence of the shivering. {nux.v}. Sensation of heat of face, with shiver on the rest of the body. {nux.v}. Heat of face with coldness of the lower parts of the body. {nux.v}. Small intermitting pulse. {nux.v}. Vanishing pulse, with perfect consciousness. [Consbruch, l.c.] {nux.v}. After coldness of the feet dry heat of face. {nux.v}. Whilst there is inward heat of head there is chill on the outside of the head. {nux.v}. Hot cheeks with internal chilliness. {nux.v}. Redness of cheeks with heat in head and chilliness on the rest of the body (aft. 6 h.). {nux.v}. In the evening red face with shivering and coldness of limbs and thirst for beer. {nux.v}. First shivering then heat, causing anxiety; afterwards thirst for beer. {nux.v}. Fever towards evening (6 p.m.); chilliness with intermediate attacks of heat, recurring the following day at the same hour. {nux.v}. At night, along with external chilliness, sensation of internal heat, with dryness of mouth but horror of drinks. {nux.v}. Afternoon fever : chill and coldness for four hours, with blue nails; then general heat and burning in the hands, with thirst at first for water, afterwards for beer, not followed by perspiration. {nux.v}. In the evening before lying down chill, but when in bed heat of head and face. {nux.v}. After lying down in the evening strong chill, and sleep for an hour, then heat with headache, roaring in the ears and nausea (aft. 12 h.). {nux.v}. After lying down in the evening trembling and chilliness-then some heat in the face (aft. 2 h.). {nux.v}. In the morning unusual heat, with thirst for water (aft. 12 h.). {nux.v}. Attack of heat of the whole body, without redness of cheeks, with beads of sweat on the forehead and anxiety. {nux.v}. Febrile heat more inwardly; she felt as if steam and smoke exhaled from the throat; at the same time she drank a great deal. {nux.v}. Quite early, in bed, an intolerable sensation of heat either of the whole body, or especially in the cheeks, hands and feet, particularly in the palms and soles, for which he seeks with avidity for coolness (throwing off the clothes and lying in cold parts of the bed), but he cannot bear it, sometimes on account of a feeling of illness in the whole body, sometimes on account of a momentarily occurring pinching or cutting in the abdomen. {nux.v}. After lying down in the evening heat in the face, inner surface of the hands and feet. {nux.v}. External heat with red cheeks and sensation of anxious, intolerable inward heat (in spite of which he covers himself up carefully); the mouth is full of saliva, and although the lips are dry, no thirst, or only a semblance of thirst; he wishes to drink and yet rejects all fluids; he does not relish drink; - sleeplessness during the heat; he lays his arms under his head; after the heat thirst for beer. {nux.v}. At night heat without thirst and almost without sweat. {nux.v} (f-h1]. At night anxiety; in his sleep he threw off the bedclothes. {nux.v}. Violent thirst. [Matthiolus, l.c.] {nux.v}. About midnight in bed, dry heat without thirst. {nux.v}. Along with heat and full quick pulse, desire to go to bed, and thirst. {nux.v}. Inward heat, increasing every hour, with full pulse, without thirst; - then sleeplessness (aft. 8, 16 h.). {nux.v}. In the morning, when walking, in the open air, increasing heat with full pulse, without thirst; -then sleeplessness (aft. 8, 16 h.). {nux.v}. In the morning, when walking in the open air, heat of the face and of the whole body (aft. 48 h.). {nux.v}. While walking, transient heat of the face more frequently than usual. {nux.v}. Transient heat when moving. {nux.v}. Transient redness and heat of cheeks on the slightest movement and exertion. {nux.v}. Sensation of heat in the face, without externally perceptible increase of temperature. {nux.v}. Redness of cheeks in the morning after waking. {nux.v}. Heat on the head in the evening. {nux.v}. Transient heat of the face towards evening (aft. 48 h.). {nux.v}. Heat of face in the morning after rising from bed, with constipation and rumbling of flatulence in the abdomen (aft. 24 h.). {nux.v}. Red, hot cheeks, without thirst. {nux.v}. Heat of the face, in the evening in bed, and restless sleep before midnight (aft. 8 d.). {nux.v}. Sensation of burning inward heat through the whole body (aft. 6, 12 h.). {nux.v}. Perspiration for two days (aft. 16 h.). {nux.v}. (When lying in bed and when walking quickly, ready perspiration.) {nux.v}. (Perspiration when moving about in the room.) {nux.v}. (Perspiration in the room, going off in the open air) (aft. 72 h.). {nux.v}. Clammy perspiration on the forehead when walking in the open air. {nux.v}. Sweat on the affected side of the face, during the semilateral headache. {nux.v}. Profuse perspiration. [Junghauss, Diss. de. nuce vomica, Hal., 1770.] {nux.v}. Il-smelling perspiration all night. {nux.v}. Fetid perspiration. [Wiel, l.c.] {nux.v}. Cold sweat. [Matthiolus, l.c.] {nux.v}. On the occurrence of cold perspiration all the pains are allayed. {Consbruch, 1.c.] {nux.v}. Sweat on one side of the head, scalp and face (aft. 10 h.). {nux.v}. Fetid sweat on the side. {nux.v}. Il-smelling sweat on one side. {nux.v}. In the morning when waking and sleeping perspiration, especially of the upper parts, then drawing pain in the left side (aft. 16 h.). {nux.v}. Quite early in the morning (at 3 a.m.) perspiration, especially under the nose, on the forehead (on the hairy scalp), in the nape, on the neck, in the scrobiculus cordis, and between the thighs, with anxious feeling of heat and dryness of the tip of the tongue, hard palate and lips, without desire for drink. {nux.v}. After midnight perspiration. {nux.v}. In the morning, from 2 a.m., sweat during sleep, but on awaking (from time to time) only slight transpiration all over. {nux.v}. Morning sweat. {nux.v}. In the morning in bed, after waking, profuse general sweat (but not on the head or face) (aft. 3 d.). {nux.v}. Slight, general perspiration (but not on the face) night and morning, smelling like damp (mouldy) straw. {nux.v}. Night sweat of a sour smell. {nux.v} [f:h1]. In the morning, about 5 o'clock, after waking, she commences to perspire, for several mornings. {nux.v}. During the morning sweat, simple pain of all the joints he is lying on. {nux.v}. During the morning sweat, inclination to vomit. {nux.v}. During the morning sweat, on the least exposure, bellyache, as from taking cold. {nux.v}. Under the bed clothes great heat and perspiration, but on slight exposure and letting the air come under the bedclothes, shivering. {nux.v} (f-h1]. After the perspiration chill and then again sweat. {nux.v}. In the morning on waking, general perspiration, with internal heat of face and hands, without thirst. {nux.v}. After the morning sweat great thirst for small beer. {nux.v}. Frequent attacks of perspiration, followed by dry heat. {nux.v}. During and after great anxiety, profuse sweat. {nux.v}. Anxiety which causes perspiration, at least on the forehead. {nux.v}. Only internal heat, caused by anxiety, followed by sweat on the forehead (aft. some h.). {nux.v}. After the anxiety, nausea and rapid breathing, then dry cough caused by the nausea, inclination to vomit and vomiting. {nux.v}. Restlessness with very dilatable pupils (aft. 56 h.). {nux.v}. In the evening after lying down, anxiety, then after midnight perspiration. {nux.v}. Anxiety; he cannot remain quiet in any one place. {nux.v} (f-h1]. In the evening when walking, anxiety, oppression and as if he were drunk. {nux.v}. In the morning on waking, and in the afternoon (at 5 p.m.), anxiety and anxious solicitude, as if something important were to be feared. {nux.v} (f-h1]. Anxiety and anguish as if he had committed some crime. {nux.v} (f-h1]. Great anxiety; he cannot rest in any place, and would rather die. {nux.v}. After midnight very violent palpitation of the heart with extreme anxiety, which urges him to commit suicide (aft. 5 h.). {nux.v}. She deems the pain she suffers intolerable and will rather take her own life. {nux.v}. Anxiety with impulse to commit suicide. {nux.v}. (Suicide; she throws herself from a height.) {nux.v}. Extraordinary anxiety. {nux.v}. Great anxiety. [Strandberg, l.c.] {nux.v}. Extreme anxiety. [F. Hoffmann, |.c.] {nux.v}. Intolerable anxiety, for an hour. [Consbruch, I.c.] {nux.v}. He fears death. {nux.v}. She thinks herself near death. {nux.v}. Wrapped up in sorrow and care. {nux.v}. Sadness. {nux.v}. (During the sadness she cannot weep.) {nux.v}. He is apprehensive, frightened, and readily starts, whilst his head is as if intoxicated and giddy. {nux.v}. On seeing some irritating object she has a shock through the legs and through the whole body; she is almost insensible for an hour. {nux.v}. Pains are not borne without loud whining and lamenting, mixed with reproaches and scolding. {nux.v}. She cannot get over the smallest evil. {nux.v}. Anxious solicitude and inconsolableness, which breaks out in loud weeping complaints and reproaches, and sometimes passes into continual groaning, with very hot red cheeks, without thirst. {nux.v}. Anxious solicitude and irresolution. {nux.v}. Anxiety from suspicious and timorous solicitude, especially in the hours after midnight. {nux.v}. She groans and sighs in a lamentable manner, without giving any reason for doing so. {nux.v}. He weeps when any one does the slightest thing he dislikes. {nux.v}. She is disposed to weep peevishly. {nux.v}. She weeps aloud and sobs (aft. 3 h.). {nux.v}. She cannot bear the least contradiction, nor suffer the most reasonable representations to induce her to alter her conduct; they put her beside herself. {nux.v}. He is peevishly solicitous, takes everything amiss, and readily breaks out into scolding and abuse (aft. 2, 3 h.). {nux.v}. She is much disposed to scolding crossness. {nux.v}. Angry peevishness, angry disposition (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. Very much given to reproach others severely for their faults. {nux.v} (f-h1]. Scolding, reproaches, abuse, jealous invectives, mixed with indelicate expressions-then soon howling and loud weeping. {nux.v}. Scolding humour developing into acts. {nux.v}. He obstinately opposes what others wish (aft. 1 h.). {nux.v}. He is hasty, looks malignantly at any one who asks him anything, without answering, just as if he must control himself in order to avoid becoming coarse; it seems as if he would like to strike any one in the face who speaks a word to him, so irritable and uncontrollable is his disposition. {nux.v}. He feels everything too strongly. {nux.v}. Over-sensitiveness to impressions of the senses; he cannot bear strong odours and bright light. {nux.v}. He cannot bear any noise or speaking; music and singing affect him strongly. {nux.v}. Over tender, soft disposition; music affects him to tears. {nux.v} (f-h1]. The slightest step, and the smallest shaking of the floor is felt by her painfully, intolerably. {nux.v}. Hypochondriacal disposition after dinner, and still more after supper. {nux.v}. Hypochondriacal sadness. {nux.v}. Dejected peevishness. {nux.v}. He puckers up his forehead in wrinkles and crosses his arms. {nux.v} (f-h1]. Quietness, as if everything were disagreeable to him. {nux.v}. Quietude and wrapped up in himself, slow flow of ideas. {nux.v}. She seeks rest and quiet. {nux.v}. Ennui; time seems to him intolerably long (in the first h.). {nux.v}. No inclination for any work. {nux.v}. Lazy about all undertakings and business; she is immediately fatigued. {nux.v}. He has a complete horror of work, and yet does not dislike movement (aft. 2 h.). {nux.v}. He dawdles and is irresolute. {nux.v}. Irresolution, constant hesitation in his intentions. {nux.v}. She wishes to do much, but thinks she will not succeed. {nux.v}. He thinks that everything will go wrong. {nux.v}. Everything goes wrong with him (everything goes contrary) (aft. 6 h.). {nux.v}. He has no patience for work. {nux.v}. He acts awkwardly and stupidly; he knocks himself or upsets things (aft. 10 h.). {nux.v}. Something, he knows not what, hinders him, especially in scientific occupations. {nux.v}. Indisposition to intellectual occupations the blood mounts to the head-until towards evening. {nux.v}. In the morning, dread of those literary occupations in which he must think for himself and unfold ideas from his own mind in order either to commit them to writing or to express them orally; but reading and learning by heart are not distasteful to him (aft. 16h.). {nux.v}. He can with difficulty collect his thoughts. {nux.v}. Incapable of thinking properly, he often makes mistakes in speaking, seeks the words with an effort and makes use of inappropriate expressions; he makes mistakes regarding weights and measures. {nux.v}. He is apt to make mistakes in speaking and writing, leaves out syllables and whole words (aft. 6, 12 h.). {nux.v}. On account of an excessive flow of ideas he is scarcely conscious, in the morning after rising (aft. 10 h.). {nux.v}. Clear consciousness of his existence; delicate, strong, proper feeling of right and wrong. {nux.v} O OLEANDER (Nerium Oleander)

Inflammatory diseases complicated with great nervous hyperesthesia and subdelirium ; coma vigil ; subsultus and fibrillary palpitations. Very nervous, tremulous, restless. Hysteria : globus hystericus ; nervous excitement of heart ; simulating tetanic spasms ; spasms with fainting ; crying one moment and the next bursts out in uncontrollable laughter ; coldness and faintness. insensibility ; scolding until she falls unconscious. Complains of pain all over body without locating it in any particular place ; depression and ill feeling even unto fainting ; sudden rush of blood to head, with staring eyes, closed set jaws, followed by rapid confused talking, deathly paleness of. face, profuse sweat about head. θ Hysteria. Before attack constriction of throat, great restlessness, oppression of chest, impatience, and intolerance of bedcovers ; attacks occur every half or every hour and last about fifteen minutes, during which she remains entirely conscious ; suddenly becomes rigid, distortion of. eyes, twitching of face, contraction of fingers ; respiration becomes suspended, the throat swells, mouth is opened, head retracted and body tossed about in her violent efforts to get air ; attacks end with belching and twitching of various parts, followed by crawling sensation in. arms ; during the attacks bends herself backwards and cries out "now it is over, now my breath is gone." θ Spasms. Face pale and anxious ; extraordinary prostration and vertigo ; indescribable pain in limbs and entire inability to move arms and legs ; skin dry ; all secretions and excretions suppressed ; lies stretched out in bed, motionless, with arms tightly held to. sides ; after several minutes, distortion of eyes, slight twitchings around nose and mouth, head repeatedly and rapidly drawn downward and backward, alæ nasi at one time contracted, then expanded ; after a deep inspiration respiration ceases for one half to two minutes, then. thorax is alternately violently contracted and expanded, during which several short and rapid respirations occur and pulse beat becomes irregular ; clonic cramps in abdominal muscles, recti abdominus being elevated and depressed like the motions of a snake ; all the muscular. movements are in a certain direction, either from above downward or in opposite direction ; finally similar convulsive movements in extremities, fingers and toes first affected, then parts near trunk ; paroxysm lasts five to six minutes, with consciousness and loss of. sensation ; epistaxis ; stitches in sides of chest ; severe retching ; involuntary nodding of head ; neither pinching, sticking nor shaking makes the least impression upon her ; pulse 65 ; pupils dilated, fixed ; menses absent ; whole attack lasts. forty-eight hours and is followed by an intermission of ten to fifteen minutes. Convulsions : tetanic spasms ; in consequence of albuminuria in fifth week of an attack of scarlet fever ; from uremic poisoning. Paroxysms set in at night, or in open air, with pulmonary spasms, or succeeded by headache. θ Syncope. Purely neuralgic pains or functional disorders, arising from taking cold. Paroxysms last five to six minutes. Alternately : thorax violently contracted and expanded. Every half hour : wakes and throws off covering. Attacks of spasms : every half or every hour, last about fifteen minutes. Nightly : copious, watery diarrhœa. Whole spasm lasts forty-eight hours ; followed by intermission of ten to fifteen minutes. In fifth week of an attack of scarlet fever : convulsions. Right : sensation in temple as if a cord were drawn and tightened ; detonation in ear like report of cannon ; redness of cheek without heat ; burning heat frequently only on side. Left : paleness of cheek which feels hot ; pain under breast ; pimples on upper arm. From above downward, or opposite direction : all muscular movements. As if she was falling from a height ; as if he was turned about so rapidly that he perceives the current of air produced by the motion ; coldness as if from cold poultices on head ; headache as if from a heavy weight ; as if a cord was frequently drawn and. tightened as if to cut head in two ; as if a nail was pressed in occiput, of which point pierces brain ; rushing in ears as from a strong wind or as from wing of a bird ; facial muscles as if too short ; movement of lower jaw as if chewing ; nausea as if from pit of. stomach ; as if everything in abdomen was constricted ; as if menses would appear ; as if larynx suddenly closed upon breath ; constriction in throat as from vapors of sulphur ; parts on which one lies feel as if dislocated and sprained ; as if cold air was. blowing on him. Pain : under left breast ; all over body. Indescribable pain : in limbs. Stitches : in anus to bladder ; in sides of chest. Pinching pains : in abdomen. Gnawing pain : in chest. Cramp : in lung ; in abdominal muscles. Aching pain : in head ; in upper and lower limbs. Drawing pains : during menses. Compressing pain : in head over root of nose. Pressive, burning pains : in stomach. Burning : in urethra. Soreness : of thorax under arms. Heat : in face ; in throat. Dryness : of throat. Constriction : of chest ; of larynx and trachea ; of throat. Drawing and dragging : towards genitals. Tightness : of chest. Tension : in back part of head and neck ; in facial muscles. Heaviness : of head. Fulness : in pit of stomach. Oppression : in pit of stomach ; of chest. Restlessness : in lower extremities. Trembling : around heart. Crawling : in point of nose ; in arms. Intolerable titillation : in genitals. Pressing, itching, biting : in eyes. Coldness : in head ; on tibia. Purely nervous diseases ; hysteria. TOUCH. PASSIVE MOTION. INJURIES. [45] [Mosch.]. Pressure : on thorax, soreness under arms. Neither pinching, sticking nor shaking makes the least impression upon her in spasm. Scratching : causes bleeding of pimples. Suitable to spoiled sensitive natures and hysteric women. Child, æt. 3 ; subject to attacks lasting from five to ten days ; urinary disorders. Boy, æt. 5 ; pale but strong, during termination of an attack of whooping cough ; spasms of larynx. Girl, æt. 13 ; delicate, slender, menses not yet appeared, had diarrhœa for which Cham. and Rheum were given ; spasms. Girl, æt. 16 ; suffering from amenorrhœa and beginning chlorosis ; threatened paralysis of lungs during typhoid fever. Girl, æt. 17 ; weak, menstruated but once, a year ago ; during her twelfth year had spasms resulting from fright, relieved by Ignat. ; spasms returned three days ago after vexation and were accompanied by retching and vomiting of bile, for which Cham. Was. prescribed ; spasms. Man, æt. 40 ; lawyer, has been dyspeptic ; incontinence of urine. Man, æt. 43 ; married ; diabetes with impotency. Woman, æt. 72 ; heart affection. Old lady ; palpitation of heart.

mental_emotional.text: Uncontrollable laughter. Scolding. Anxiety with palpitation; starting as if frightened. Sexual hypochondriasis

Excitement as from alcohol. Great absence of mind, sometimes with confused speech. Preoccupation, during which the patient talks to himself, and gesticulates, as if bereft of reason. Bustling activity, with weakness, so that everything falls from hands. Fretful mind. Cries one moment, uncontrollable laughter the next. Complaints and lamentations on account of excessive sufferings, with inability to indicate the part affected; when questioned, patient complains still more urgently, but without saying where he suffers. Apprehension of death, and excessive timidity about dying. Hypochondriacal anxiety, sometimes with palpitation of heart. Exceedingly quarrelsome humour and great irascibility, with passion, proceeding even to fury. Very violent anger, raves and scolds till mouth dry, lips blue, eyes staring, face deathly pale, and she falls unconscious. Loss of memory.

Great anxiety with palpitation of heart ; feeling of anxious expectancy. Hypochondriacal anxiety and ill humor. Scolding ; keeps it up till her lips turn blue, eyes stare, and she falls down fainting. Great dread of death, with paleness of face, fainting ; talks only of approaching death. Hypochondriacal complaints having their origin in sexual system. Complains without knowing what ails him, with anguish, palpitation, etc. Tearful vexation and peevishness, with violent quarrelling, even to most extreme malice and rage. Great bustling, during which everything falls out of his hand from weakness. Thoughtlessness, with foolish gestures and complaints of pain. Sudden loss of memory, with complete inability to collect his senses. Great tendency to get frightened, trembling, palpitation, of heart, and dread of death. Sensation as if she was falling from a height, with stupefaction. Sensation as if he was turned about so rapidly that he perceives current of air produced by motion. Vertigo : as soon as head is moved ; on moving eyelids, > in open air ; on stooping, going off on rising ; accompanied by a kind of loathing ; with nausea ; vomiting, desire for black coffee and to lie down ; with staring eyes and sort of spasm in. mouth, preventing speech, though he sees and hears everything ; with sudden fainting ; with violent rush of blood to head, > in open air ; with stupefaction ; congestion of blood to head ; fits of tetanus and fainting ; dimness of sight.

head.text: Compressive pain over root of nose. Pressure on top of head. Vertigo on least motion; sensation as if falling from a great height. Scalp sensitive. Sounds in ears as from the report of a cannon

Vertigo and wavering before eyes, on least movement of head or eyelids. Vertigo stooping, > rising. Vertigo, even to loss of consciousness, or with fainting. Vertigo, with sensation of falling. Vertigo, with nausea and vomiting, want to lie down, and desire for coffee. Dizziness, as from intoxication. Headache, with nausea and vomiting, which compels lying down. Heaviness in head. Compressive and stunning headache, esp. just above root of nose; with nausea in evening, > moving head, and in room, > in open air. Cramp-like and tensive drawing in head, and esp. in occiput, extending nearly to nape of neck; with nausea, in evening, when sitting in room, and when becoming cold, > in open air, and when getting warm. Congestion in head. Congestion of blood to head, heaviness of head. Aching and boring pain in occiput, as if a nail were driven into brain; sitting in a room.

Compressing headache, especially right over root of nose. Tension in back part of head and neck, with nausea, < in evening, when sitting in room and when becoming cold, > in open air and when getting warm. Aching pain in head, with coldness as from cold poultices. Congestion of blood to head, heaviness of head. Headache with nausea, compelling one to lie down. Stupefying, compressing headache, mostly in forehead, with nausea in evening, < when moving head and in room, > in open air. Hysterical headache with fainting spasms and sense of constriction of chest, chilliness all over, inclination to involuntary stools and copious flow of colorless urine ; great restlessness of lower extremities, pressive headache as if from a heavy weight in. head ; sensation in right temple as if a cord was frequently drawn and tightened as if to cut head in two ; pain as if a nail was pressed in occiput, of which point pierces brain ; < in room, > in fresh air.

stomach_abdomen.text: Desire for black coffee, stimulants. Aversion to food. Everything tastes flat. With stomach symptoms, anxiety in chest. Distended. Faints when eating. Abdomen greatly distended. Spasmodic, nervous hiccough (Hydrocy ac; Sulph ac; Ignat; Cajap)

Putrid taste of food. Great desire for beer or brandy. Thirst. Aversion to food. Faints while eating. Spasmodic hiccough. Violent eructations, sometimes with nausea. Sudden attacks of nausea; at sight or thought of food. Nausea, which mounts from epigastrium, with retraction of navel, and cramp-like pains. Violent vomiting, esp. in morning, and often accompanied by pain in stomach and chest, and distension of epigastrium. Aching in stomach. Sensation of fulness and obstruction in region of stomach and epigastrium, sometimes with uneasiness, after a moderate meal. Smarting, burning sensation of excoriation, in region of stomach, after a meal. Hysterical abdominal spasms. Attacks of painful contraction in umbilical region, with suspended respiration. Sensation of tension in abdomen, as if clothes were too tight, accompanied by an anxiety which neither permits attention to any kind of labour, nor continuance in one place, but compels constant running from side to side. Incarceration of flatus.

APPETITE, THIRST. DESIRES, AVERSIONS. [14] [Mosch.]. Great desire for beer or brandy. Aversion to food ; the sight of it makes her sick ; eructations ; vomiting ; pressive, burning pains and distension of stomach. Sits down to her meals and faints dead away from the little extra amount of food taken into stomach. Heartburn with palpitation. Eructations with hot saliva in mouth. Spasmodic nervous hiccough, especially if occurring in hysterical subjects. Nausea when thinking of food. Sudden nausea as from pit of stomach ; umbilicus being drawn in. Vomiting of food, then subsequent vomiting and more and more vomiting. Sensation of fulness and oppression in pit of stomach with qualmishness, < from eating. Hematemesis ; becomes pulseless, collapsed. Persistent troubles of digestive functions in susceptible hysterical subjects, with palpitation of heart, dyspnœa and prostration ; is afraid to lie down for fear of death. Excessive distension of abdomen, with pinching pains. Great tympanitis in hysterical women, with fainting. Incarcerated flatus. Sensation as if everything in abdomen was constricted, compelling one to move about, cannot do any work, or remain quiet anywhere. Hysterical, abdominal spasms.

male.text: Violent desire; involuntary emissions. Impotence, associated with diabetes (Coca). Premature senility. Nausea and vomiting after coition

Great increase of sexual desire, sometimes with insupportable tickling in the parts, or tensive pains in penis. A small retracted penis in an old man suddenly attains its former size; an impotent man became natural. Impotence occasioned by a cold; preceding diabetes. Painful involuntary pollutions without erection. Erection, with burning pain in urethra. Erections with desire to urinate. Nausea and vomiting after coition.

Violent sexual excitement, with intolerable titillation in genitals. Erection, with desire to urinate. Involuntary emissions : painful ; without erections. Impotence : occasioned by a cold, preceding diabetes mellitus.

female.text: Menses too early, too profuse, with disposition to faint (Nux m; Veratr). Sexual desire, with intolerable titillation in parts. Drawing and pushing in the direction of the genitals; sensation as if menses appear

Violent sexual desire. Menses: too early and too profuse, with drawing pain; with intolerable titillation in genitals; great disposition to faint. Desire greatly excited (in an old woman). Drawing, and sensation of bearing down towards hypogastrium and the genital organs during menses. Dysmenorrhœa with fainting. Bearing down as if menses would appear. During pregnancy she complains much, but of nothing in particular. 17.

Violent sexual desire. Menses : too early and too profuse, with drawing pain ; with intolerable titillation in genitals ; great disposition to faint. Drawing and dragging towards genitals ; feeling as if menses would appear. PREGNANCY. PARTURITION. LACTATION. [24] [Mosch.]. She complains much, but of nothing in particular. θ Pregnancy.

urinary.text: Urine : clear as water and very copious ; scanty, thick like yeast. Burning in urethra, with great nervous excitability. Urine during day normal in appearance, but at night dark red, very offensive and depositing a mucous sediment on standing. θ Incontinence of urine. (In sick :) Insatiable thirst, great emaciation, constipation, clammy mouth, frequent discharge of large quantities of urine containing sugar. θ Impotence.

respiratory.text: Tightness of chest, is obliged to take a deeper breath. Sudden constriction of larynx and trachea. Difficult respiration; chest oppressed; hysterical spasm of chest; asthma. Spasm of glottis. Impending paralysis of lungs. Asthma, with intense anxiety, fear, and smothering sensation. Cough ceases, mucus cannot be expectorated. Globus hystericus

VOICE AND LARYNX. TRACHEA AND BRONCHIA. [25] [Mosch.]. Sudden sensation in upper part of larynx as if it closed upon the breath. Sudden sensation of constriction in larynx and trachea as from vapors of sulphur. Spasmus glottidis. Spasmodic constriction of larynx and chest, especially when feeling cold. θ Asthma. Hysterical cases, with impending paralysis of pneumogastric. θ Asthma thymicum. Laryngismus stridulus in nervous women and children. Often useful in last stage of croup when all sorts of remedies have been useful in vain. Difficult respiration. Spasm of diaphragm. Asthma in hysteric individuals and in children. Oppression of chest and suffocative fits as from vapors of sulphur, commencing with desire to cough and getting < until one despairs of getting over paroxysms. θ Asthma. Spasms of larynx during whooping cough ; long crowing inspiration as in laryngismus stridulus, followed by several normal respirations, when crowing recurred, accompanied by anxiety and restlessness. Spasms of glottis or bronchi ; spasmodic contraction of respiratory organs threatening suffocation during croup. Sudden constriction of larynx as from fumes of sulphur, accompanied by spasmodic twitching and movements of arms and legs or by tetanic rigidity of body. θ Asthma. Severe, dry cough, < in morning and pain under l. breast on coughing. Last stage when expectoration has nearly ceased ; spasmodic cough, with vertigo and constriction of chest and trachea. θ Pertussis.

chest_heart.text: Hysterical palpitation. Trembling around heart. Weak pulse and fainting

Anxious palpitation of heart. Palpitation; dyspnœa; prostration; nervousness-says, "I shall die, I know I shall die.". Sensation of trembling around heart, with constriction in whole chest. Prostration followed by threatened collapse.

Tightness of chest ; is obliged to take a deeper breath. Violent spasms of chest, in which it seems she must almost die ; turns blue in face and foams at mouth. Hysterical spasms of chest ; nervous, suffocative constriction, especially on becoming cold. A kind of cramp in lung, beginning with an inclination to cough, gradually increasing, making desperate. Gnawing pain in chest, with a sense of suffocation. Paralysis of lungs, when there is loud rattling of mucus and the patient is restless ; especially indicated after typhoid fever ; pulse grows less and less strong, and finally patient goes into a syncope. Hysteric palpitations, or when nervous or muscular energy of heart is weakened by great mental exertion, anxiety or emotions. Palpitation of heart ; dyspnœa ; prostration ; nervousness ; says "I shall die, I know I shall die." θ Dyspnœa. Nervous palpitation of heart, with copious, watery urine. Sensation of trembling around heart, with constriction in whole chest, almost suffocation ; felt continually obliged to take a breath ; pulse 88, respiration oppressed ; arcus senilis, not very marked. θ Heart. affection. Pulse : full and accelerated, with ebullitions. Great want of blood, with weak pulse and fainting. Prostration followed by threatened collapse. θ Angina. Soreness of thorax under arms, when pressing on it.

worse_from.text: cold. The open air is felt very, very cold
